actors.role: Produces a proposed block in a consensus round.
actors.source_finding: S1 business_vocabulary Proposer; S3 dependency EXISTING

actors.role: Records committed blocks and is authoritative for committed history.
Receives the initial minted supply at bootstrap and owns the mint wallet thereafter.
Decides which blocks are proposed; authoritative for a proposed block until commit.

bm_entities.description: The authoritative, ordered, append-only ledger of committed blocks.
bm_entities.store_model: An append-only record of committed blocks plus a pointer to the most recent committed block (the head).
bm_entities.source_finding: S2 entities Chain; S3 gaps chain store

bm_entities.description: A committed unit of the ledger carrying the transactions of its round, linked to its predecessor.
bm_entities.store_model: An element of the chain; its content is hashed as its signature at commit.

bm_entities.description: The first block, created once at bootstrap, carrying the initial mint transaction.
bm_entities.store_model: The single first element of the chain.

events.trigger: The one-time bootstrap, before the consensus loop runs.
events.lifecycle_meaning: Establishes the chain and the initial monetary state.

events.trigger: A proposer produces a block in a round.
events.lifecycle_meaning: A candidate block exists; not yet authoritative.

events.trigger: A proposed block is committed to the canonical chain.
events.lifecycle_meaning: The block and its transactions become authoritative and immutable.
events.source_finding: S1 business_events Block Committed; S3 REUSE (blockchain::EV_BLOCK_COMMITTED_V0)

events.trigger: After a block is committed, balances are recomputed from the committed transactions.
events.lifecycle_meaning: Wallet balances become consistent with the canonical committed history.
events.source_finding: S1 business_events Balance Reconciled; S3 REUSE (blockchain::EV_BALANCE_RECONCILED_V0)

capability_graph.capability: Commit a proposed block to the canonical chain
Store the canonical chain ledger and track its head
Validate a block's predecessor link before commit
Bootstrap the genesis chain and initialise the supply

capability_graph.notes: reused as the genesis mint step

capability_graph.capability: Announce that a block was committed
capability_graph.notes: existing event, emitted by the new commit capability

capability_graph.capability: Record a committed block using the existing block schema

dependency_graph.source_finding: S3 dependency EXISTING; blockchain::WF_PROPOSE_BLOCK_V0 produces the proposed block
S3 dependency REUSE; blockchain::WF_MINT_V0 mints the genesis supply
S3 architectural_observations; balances derived from committed transactions (blockchain::ENTITY_TRANSACTION_V0)

constraint_register.constraint: Closed monetary system — no supply enters or leaves except by the system's own rules.
The chain is immutable — a committed block cannot be altered or removed.
Genesis supply is fixed at 1,000,000 BachiCoin, minted to the Genesis Actor at bootstrap.
Exactly one genesis block exists per chain, and genesis executes exactly once and is never replayed.
Total supply is conserved and equals 1,000,000 BachiCoin (no minting or burning after genesis).
Every committed block has exactly one predecessor, except the genesis block; a block cannot be committed twice.
The canonical chain is authoritative; a proposed block is not authoritative until committed.
Wallet balances are derived from committed transactions and reconciled on-chain post-commit; the chain does not own balance state independently.

gap_register.capability: Commit a proposed block to the canonical chain
Store the canonical chain ledger and track its head
Validate a block's predecessor link before commit
Bootstrap the genesis chain and initialise the supply

design_decisions.decision: The commit path is a new capability that runs downstream of block proposal and records a proposed block as canonical.
design_decisions.rationale: No commit capability exists; the chain plugs in directly after proposal.

design_decisions.decision: The canonical chain is an append-only store with a head pointer; committed blocks are never mutated or removed.
design_decisions.rationale: Immutability and single-predecessor invariants require durable, append-only persistence.

design_decisions.decision: A block's content signature is computed by a new block-canonical hash transform that reuses the generic keccak primitive.
design_decisions.rationale: No block hash exists; cryptographic linkage is required at commit.
design_decisions.constraints_imposed: Signature computed at commit as the block's identity.

design_decisions.decision: The predecessor link is validated against the current chain head before a block is committed.
design_decisions.rationale: Enforces exactly one predecessor per committed block.
design_decisions.constraints_imposed: Commit rejects a block whose predecessor does not match the head.

design_decisions.decision: Genesis is a one-time bootstrap that reuses minting to initialise the supply before the consensus loop runs.
design_decisions.source_finding: S3 authoring_decisions AUTHOR_NEW genesis + REUSE mint
design_decisions.rationale: Genesis-once invariant; reuse minting rather than a new monetary mechanism.

design_decisions.decision: Wallet balances are derived and reconciled on-chain after commit; the chain does not own balance state.
design_decisions.rationale: The human's authority decision at the S2 design review.
design_decisions.constraints_imposed: Balances derived from committed transactions; reconciliation is a post-commit process.

design_decisions.decision: Every proposed block is committed directly this increment; attestation and finalization are deferred.
design_decisions.constraints_imposed: No rejection path; no attest/finalize step.

authoring_scope.capability: Commit a proposed block to the canonical chain
Store the canonical chain ledger and track its head
Validate a block's predecessor link before commit
Bootstrap the genesis chain and initialise the supply
